items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Friends, it's been too long. I gather you were also listening in on that conversation about the Golden Anthem's last work?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: What did you mean by Daemons?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: "Resentful of the Order's verdict, they committed to paper a curse upon Rinascita and Ragunna."

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: "Those favored by the Divinity were the most willing to raise the blade of rebellion, for they alone knew how to make Divinity bleed and doctrines crumble."

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: "Though they died in exile to a great fire, their resentment festered in their music and poetry, transforming into horrors known as Daemons."

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: "Like the fire that dragged them to hell, these Daemons will spread throughout the land and burn all of Ragunna."

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: This article was recently published in the Ragunna Literary Journal... Quite the perspective, wouldn't you say? Vengeance borne of resentment—such dark and twisted characters are hard to resist.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Perhaps someone unwittingly played that tune just now and opened the Plushies to possession by the Daemons.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Then again, it's all conjecture. Those in the Poets Society died years ago, and who's to say what really happened?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Now, if those vengeful spirits really do exist... well, out of all the patrons here, they went straight for you, {PlayerName}. Curious and curiouser...

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: By the way, it just so happens that I made a copy of the Poet Society's last work.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Here, take it. Consider it a little souvenir from your... spirited encounter. Maybe it'll prove useful later.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Goodbye friends, a change of venue is in order. I must finish my play.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: {PlayerName}, Carlotta, it's been too long!

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: You really must adore that song. I remember hearing you sing it when I was little.

options.text: You knew each other as children?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: When I was a child, whenever I passed by Ciaccona's house, I'd always hear music drifting from the courtyard. Lively, spirited and so full of emotion...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Oh, I remember it well! Those two guards of yours were like giants from Septimont, easily two hundred pounds of pure muscle!

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Back then Ciaccona's family conducted the Order's choir.

options.text: Then why is Ciaccona a wandering bard?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: How should I put it... Well, as they say, families are always rising and falling.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: But a song is a song whether sung in a grand hall or beneath the open sky. As for family matters... let the past stay where it belongs.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Anyway, the two of you came in such a hurry. I suppose you're not here to take in the music.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Oh so... So you eavesdropped on a man rambling on in C3-sharp, and then poof, out pops a "monster"?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: It is the point! I need to know what kind of music could possibly have the power to conjure up those "monsters" of yours.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Let me see... it should sound something like this! About that sharp, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I know right? We should suggest to Ms. Margherita that she add new signage: "Sing with civility, it starts with us!"

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Ahem, can we discuss restaurant etiquette later?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Of course. But I still need to know what kind of music could possibly have the power to conjure up those "monsters" of yours.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The Golden Anthem... that name should never be spoken.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: They're criminals. To conspire with them is to risk banishment at sea, or worse.

options.text: The Order of the Deep can't harm us one bit.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Don't worry. This won't be the first time we've crossed paths with the Order.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: ...Fine.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: From what you've told me, the composers of this poem cursed Rinascita spelling the fall of Ragunna.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: When the poem is played some type of horror appears. If I understand correctly, that's what attacked you and Carlotta.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: If you ask me this story is a right load of codswallop. I don't believe a simple little poem could curse Rinascita.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Then you...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: We need to get to the heart of this poem and music mystery. Let me join you. I can't resist a good adventure.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I'll help you find the link between the music and those Daemons. But in return, I want you to hand these pages to me for safekeeping.

options.text: It's just a few sheets of paper.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Then we have a deal!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Since you believe this music summons them, how about we test it for ourselves? I've already memorized the sheet music.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: It's risky, but there's no surer way to find out, wouldn't you agree, {PlayerName}?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The final work of poets banished to distant seas and condemned to death...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: What, I wonder, did the verses forged in flames truly sing of?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Ta-da! Welcome to <te href=800010>Requiem Paradise</te>!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: This is where Ragunna's musicians lay their old instruments to rest.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: For the people of Ragunna, the sea is everything. We live and sleep by the sea, and our souls drift out to sea when we pass.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Since an instrument is an extension of the musician, it's only fitting that it's buried by the sea. May its dreams be long and sweet before returning to the waves.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I'm certain this is where the poets of the Golden Anthem laid their instruments to rest before their banishment.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: What's wrong? You seem troubled.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Is that what's been on your mind this whole time? I say, if the answers aren't coming, let it all go and clear your mind.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: To be honest... I haven't found any leads either. But worry not, this is what we bards call a "bottleneck."

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Once, I spent three days just trying to find the right words to describe a tree... couldn't do it. Not one word.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: So I stood under that tree and prayed for an apple to fall on my head.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: You know, just like in those stories where inspiration strikes after an apple hits you. And, well... at least you'd get a snack out of it.

options.text: So did an apple hit you?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Sadly, the apples weren't ripe and none fell. No free nibbles for me, unfortunately.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The apples never fell. I stood under that tree until I couldn't any longer, but still no inspiration came.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Try it!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It's a real apple...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Later, when I finally decided to leave, I looked up through the gaps in the branches and suddenly saw a blooming flower atop a cliff.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Turns out, even without an apple, inspiration may yet strike. But if I hadn't stood there, I might've missed that unexpected burst of beauty.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: So… maybe this is enough. Let's put aside heavy thoughts and continue our walk with a clear mind.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I'm starting to understand why Carlotta calls you <ano=Sunlit>"Topaz"</ano>.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Is that so? But I'm nowhere near as dazzling as the sun. If anything, I'd rather be like Icarus in the story.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Soaring freely in the sky, even if it means... falling in the end.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: A splendid retaliation. I witnessed all of your performance!

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Like everyone present, I too eagerly anticipate tomorrow's arrival.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: As expected, the Order's fingerprints are all over it. According to our inside sources...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: It was none other than Acolytes from the Order who salvaged the poets' lost works from the wreckage. They've long known of their existence and hid the poems in the Order's library.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: It's possible this was a sham all along. Choosing to make it public now likely means they're using those poems to obscure the real truth.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Now, considering the fact that the untraceable article first began circulating among poets, musicians, and playwrights alike...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I think it's fair to say its author is a regular of such secret circles. He may even be the Resonator we've speculated about.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: His collusion with the Order is why these rumors are allowed to run rampant in the streets.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I'll have someone follow this secret circle's trail and see what we can find.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: We should also share these findings with Ciaccona. As a poet, her insights would be much appreciated.
You search around but find no sign of Ciaccona.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That's strange, she was just there a moment ago.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: ...Just like when we were children. Gone without a trace.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: When she and her family disappeared without even a goodbye.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Ms. Margherita, have you seen Ciaccona?

items.speaker: Margherita
items.text: Ms. Ciaccona... Hmm, she was squirreling off towards the harbor earlier. I thought that's where you were meeting, so I didn't say a word.

items.speaker: Margherita
items.text: She did look quite down, though. Never seen her wear such a gloomy face before.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Thank you for letting us know, Ms. Margherita.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Why don't we split up and look for her at the harbor?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: A penniless bard and the Laureate roving in a foreign land. Together, we rise up against the Order of the Deep itself...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: But as long as the seeds of doubt are planted, the kindling in Ragunna will eventually ignite to forge its true gold.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: There. Now how's that for a story worth telling?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Ah! Speaking of which... Have you ever read that novel? It's about a knight on a quest to challenge a giant in the name of justice.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The knight is also accompanied by a squire. Time after time, they throw themselves at the seemingly invincible giant!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I am no Montelli, Acolyte of the Order of the Deep, or Knight of Ragunna...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: But still, I shall lead the charge! Ya-hoo! Mount up, Sir Knight! To battle we go!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Phew...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: A masked hero suddenly appeared, rescuing the bard right when she was about to be apprehended by the Order.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: But that's a story for another time.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: You know what this means for you, right?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Even with a mask on, people will still recognize the Laureate.

options.text: I told you I'd stand by your side.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I'm now a wanted criminal by the Order.

options.text: So I guess that makes us... partners in crime?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Hahaha...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Now this is what a real poet... No, wait, a real knight is supposed to be!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Well then...

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: My Lord Primus, it seems your plan has not gone quite as you'd hoped.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: You sought to revive the blasphemous Poets Society, spreading their legacy to stoke the fires of prejudice. And through my aid, summon Daemons to sow fear across Ragunna City...

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: I care not for labels of good or evil. My only care is Ragunna's order and chaos.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Fear... is but one tool among many to maintain stability.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Ah! Truly the words of one in possession of great power! Such lines are rare even in plays.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: And yet... why do you seem so restless, Lord Fenrico? This is hardly the character I expected from you.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Is it that foreign Laureate that unsettles you so? Or perhaps that insignificant Toccata girl?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Or is it... something else you seek?

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Out of respect for our partnership, some things are better left unsaid.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Of course. As you will, Lord Fenrico.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Wait... You were the one who led the Poets Society to that lithograph, weren't you?

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: I've heard whispers of your presence at Whisperwind Haven around that time. Whenever you were asked, you claimed to be seeking inspiration.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Why are you doing this?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: As you've said yourself, out of respect for our partnership, some things... are better left unsaid.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: There was no deception. Indeed, I was seeking inspiration. In the end... I found a story about birds in a cage.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: The birds, having uncovered the truth, must decide—will they choose to fly into the unknown, or remain caged within the familiar?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Ah, Lord Fenrico, a word of reminder...

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Just as the young Toccata mentioned, how long has it been since you last dreamed of your true "Divinity"?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Armed with a wooden spear and a broken helmet, the knight charged at the windmill...

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: His squire, ever loyal, trailed close behind as if becoming one with the knight. Together they charged again and again, true knights in spirit, ignoring the countless wounds they bore.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Truly astonishing... Even if you took such a script, what could you possibly accomplish, "Hecate"?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Do you feel it? The breeze from Whisperwind Haven. You can even catch the faint scent of wet grass.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Next thing you know, maybe an apple will fall on our heads. Or maybe a big gray wolf will leap out and chase a squirrel down a rabbit hole...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I don't have the Blessed Maiden's miracles, nor the powers of a hero. I'm just a wandering bard.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The only thing I can offer is to immortalize this moment. I will carry all the stories about you with me...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: By the way, I met a man at Margherita's who claims to be a playwright. You should watch out for him!

options.text: I've met a playwright before as well...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I'm not entirely sure who he really is... But my guess is that he might be the Resonator who's been working with the Order.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Here, you can have this music sheet! I'm heading off on a new mission!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: An escape mission! Starting today, I'm no longer just a wandering bard. I've got the Order hunting me now! A journey like this? It's begging to be written into a story.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Don't you worry! I still remember our promise.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: About the song I wrote you... We will meet again, and I'll sing it for you, I swear!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: After all, we're partners in crime now!
They burned in despair, resentful of the Order's verdict till their last breath. Their last work is nothing but a curse upon us all!
"With our bodies to fuel the fire, the ensuing inferno shall forge Ragunna's true gold." Is it saying Ragunna will burn to the ground??
They wanted their resentment to echo through the streets... Just by reading this, I can almost see their terrifying faces in the fire!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: These voices... they're people of Ragunna, aren't they? I think I heard Mr. C3-sharp in the mix.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: If the poets' resentful souls gave birth to Daemons, then shouldn't they sound like the poets themselves?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Those voices were just people who read or heard their work...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Perhaps the voices themselves are what causes the Daemons to manifest.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: In the legends, Daemons are born of death and curses.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: But what we've seen isn't like that, no. They're more like horrors created from peoples' words and imagination.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Of course, I'm just speculating. The voices were somewhat muddled. Perhaps I can refine the melody. This time we need to listen harder.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Let's find out what the true story really is...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: These "Bonfire Poets" were no doubt members of the Poets Society. The note says they had wandered the area looking for inspiration.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: To find their melody, we should try to follow the route they once took.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Perhaps inspiration will strike us along the way!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: This was the last stop on their route. Oh, the view just now as we soared in the sky was just breathtaking!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: What is it? Has anything come to mind?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Oh yes, how did I miss that. The wind is the finest recorder of melodies.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: It rises from the primal, churning endlessly, echoing in our ears without end. I believe the melodies left by the Poets Society were witnessed by the wind, and it will carry them to us.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Playing a tune, of course. I want to capture the mood they must have felt when they were here... I'm curious as to what they left behind.
Ciaccona plays her instrument. The wind stirs, and the melody of the Poets Society fills the air.

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: Rimbaud and Selena must have returned to Ragunna by now. That lithograph of the Sentinel's true form… it has to be here somewhere.

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: Did that gentleman guide us intentionally? Who exactly was he...

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: Backpack stuffed with papers, claiming to be a common artist traveling in search of inspiration.

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: Yet he spoke like a nobleman... But I can't afford to think about that now.

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: If not for his help, we'd still be singing praises to a false Sentinel!

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: He's right. We cannot continue to sing for the Order anymore.

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: We must enlighten the people of Ragunna. They deserve to know the truth.

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: The knights of the Order defend only the Order's interests, and so... the time has come for us to become knights for the people of Ragunna.
Earlier before, Ciaccona walked to the harbor...

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: The young birds left their cage, flapping frail wings as they fell to the ground.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I never thought his words would reach into the depths I sealed away so long ago.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Coming here implies you worry about tomorrow's outcome.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: You intend to reveal the truth of the Sentinel to the public. But that revelation... it comes with a price. One you've paid before, as a child. Remember?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: This is... the past.

items.speaker: Acolyte Interrogator
items.text: And here I thought you'd be much wiser than this.

items.speaker: Acolyte Interrogator
items.text: You know the dangers of pilgrimage, yet you all still cling to the same reckless path. Tell me, what drives such stubbornness?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: The truth has its thorns, and the Golden Anthem has already paid its price. Do you wish to relive their fate?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Is that what you're trying to tell me with your play...? But you know nothing of how the Golden Anthem responded.

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: Allow me to share the answer, Sir Acolyte. Do you remember the very first voice from the heavens? The one the people of Rinascita kept alive in their hearts as they built their homes on this land?

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: Surely you've seen <te href=800009>The Golden Bough</te>, yes? A play where people from various clans wear masks, singing and celebrating together. An occasion you now call... "Carnevale."

items.speaker: Acolyte Interrogator
items.text: And? What of it?

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: It is words and songs. We are not your enemies. What you face here is something that is hard to describe, something I call "Reverberation."

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: It exists forever, and you can never defeat it.

items.speaker: Acolyte Interrogator
items.text: Hmph. You pitiful fool. Off to the seas with that "Reverberation" you believe in so dearly.

items.speaker: Faroll
items.text: The Order of the Deep seeks to banish us to the seas because they know they can't banish the truth...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Hear that? The bell they tolled... I will be the one to carry its sound forward.

items.speaker: Middle-aged Acolyte Interrogator
items.text: Oh, the parallels... so striking. Just like the day the Poets Society boarded their Sail. You, Ciaccona Toccata, blaspheming against the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Middle-aged Acolyte Interrogator
items.text: Ha! So this is the resonance they babbled on about? After all these years, another group of fools is ready to march down the same path.

items.speaker: Middle-aged Acolyte Interrogator
items.text: Why are there always those who believe the Sentinel is some damn horse?

items.speaker: Middle-aged Acolyte Interrogator
items.text: The poets were at least clever enough to hold their tongues. But you have the nerve to parade your blasphemy through the streets. It almost makes those poets look noble by comparison.

items.speaker: Ciaccona (past)
items.text: Is that so, Sir Acolyte? I do hope you dream of this "Sentinel" of yours every night then.

items.speaker: Middle-aged Acolyte Interrogator
items.text: Spare me your sarcasm, you insolent bard! You're nothing but a mouthy relic, the last of what remains of your wretched family! Do you not repent for your crimes?!

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Ah, so you've already tasted their fate, haven't you? You and your family, banished in the name of... blasphemy!

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Such a dramatic turn of events! As cantors of the Sentinel, both the Toccata family and the Poets Society committed the same crime. Tell me, what is the connection between you and those poets?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: You're nothing but a figment of my imagination. None of this concerns you. It was a price we were willing to pay. We have no regrets.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: When I was a child, I lacked the ability to prove the Sentinel's true form. But now, with the Inverted Tower shifting and the prophesied Laureate among us...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I believe the opportunity has presented itself for the truth to be—

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Opportunity. Indeed... The moment of choice dawns once more.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Do you intend to repeat history? This time, dragging {PlayerName} along with you? Boarding the Pilgrim's Sail and clinging to hope as the storm carries you away.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Isn't this what you're afraid of?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: You've unraveled the hand behind it all... but the Order will never allow such truths to see the light.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: {PlayerName}, Ms. Carlotta, and all those you've gathered along the way will be forced to bear the consequences of your quest for the truth.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: And perhaps even that... "Blessed Maiden" whose whereabouts remain a mystery to this day.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Even so, will you two still insist on stepping onto that stage tomorrow?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Have you really made your choice? I look forward to the arrival of tomorrow, Miss Poet.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Is this Ciaccona's sheet music? She was here.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The vision I just saw seems to have been recorded from this...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Ciaccona mentioned she always writes down her melodies on manuscript paper. Could this be...?

options.text: Wait. The title of this section...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The Golden Anthem...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The timbre also matches... I believe this lute is exactly what we've been looking for.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Sometimes, even when the instruments are the same, the performer's mood can change the entire sound.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: For example, this lute doesn't seek to boast. It speaks softly, like a traveler sharing a story over tea in the warmth of an afternoon.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The true purpose of music is to express what words cannot.

options.text: It certainly has the style of a poet.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: {PlayerName}, if there's something you want to say but can't put into words, if you sing it, maybe I'll understand.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: But I haven't even heard you sing yet.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Me? Oh, perhaps another time. Once all this is settled, I'll perform for you. Be sure to listen closely then!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Now, it's in the lute's hands. I hope this melody holds a tale worth telling.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: This song... it's the one she performed last time.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The melody is the same, but why does it sound so sad this time?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Is this form of the Sentinel the same as the one you saw at Avinoleum, {PlayerName}?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The majestic steed carved on the lithograph looks the same as the Sentinel you saw before.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: So the poets were indeed banished for uncovering the Order's secrets.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Even under interrogation, they held their silence. It wasn't until they met this mysterious man and learned the truth that they chose to become "Knights of Ragunna."

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Resentment and curses are not songs of their own style. As for the true mastermind, we still have no direct proof linking him to the Order.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: We must confront his lies and put an end to these Daemon incidents...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: But once the truth of the Golden Anthem and Imperator is revealed, it will no longer be just a matter of Daemons... But... is now the right time?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The will of the people is strong. This mastermind is so sure that the lies about the Sentinel will go undiscovered, and that no one will easily believe the truth.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The truth exists to be uncovered. All we need is to plant the seeds of doubt in the people's hearts and let them take root and grow.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Yes... Let's get going then.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: We'll go back to Margherita's. It's where our story began... and it's where it must end.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: {PlayerName}, fancy running into you here.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Won't you dine with me? Ms. Margherita asked me to sample her newest pizza, but my goodness, these portions...

items.speaker: Street Musician
items.text: Did you hear what the Order's Acolytes found while clearing the abandoned library? A manuscript of a poem with an accompanying music sheet.

items.speaker: Street Musician
items.text: The public has already caught wind of its contents. Did you know it was signed as Golden Anthem?

items.speaker: Street Musician
items.text: They say it was their last work, written aboard the Pilgrim's Sail. I reckon the original finder fished it out of the sea and put it in the library, not knowing what it was.

items.speaker: Street Poet
items.text: Shhh... quiet. They're criminals. Best not to mention them in public.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Shh, that name is not to be spoken of in Ragunna.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Simply put, the Golden Anthem was once a renowned Poets Society.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Their three members were once the Sentinel's cantors, but the Order banished them for insulting the Divinity.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Allegedly, their Pilgrim's Sail caught fire and none on board survived, and thus their last known work disappeared.

items.speaker: Street Poet
items.text: Well, since the manuscript is no longer a secret, how does the tune sound?

items.speaker: Street Musician
items.text: Give me a second, let me think...

items.speaker: Street Musician
items.text: Ah! Hear that? It's exactly what they're playing now!
Well... it would appear they were possessed by Daemons, just as this very article suggests.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: In her world, poetry and music hold equal sway... Shh, listen carefully...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Follow the song to find her!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Such a beautiful voice, isn't it?

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: You... You're the Laureate! And-and you are...?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: My name is Ciaccona, a—

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: —a friend of mine.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Don't be scared. We're only here so that you can finish your game of hide and seek.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: Hide and seek... right. Myron and the others are waiting for me. Sorry, I’ve caused you trouble...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: How-how about a little chat first? Something's troubling you, I can tell.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: What? Of course not. The ceremony is coming up and the choir still has so much to rehearse. I-I'm fine...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Ah, young ones today have so much talent... If I had the chance, I'd love to learn how to sing hymns.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: You know, our Laureate here definitely knows how to sing hymns.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: It's just... before the performance, the choir has to listen to the same music, recite the same lyrics, and do the same rehearsals every day.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: What's more, the hymn we're practicing... it was composed by bad people. Teacher calls them "heretic poets."

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: Myron and the others never complain. They're always eager to sing praises to the Sentinel on stage.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: But now, their dreams are all dashed because of me. Myron, Patero, and Shia's voices are in perfect harmony. But I can never keep up.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: And I don't like this hymn. Myron and the others are too young to realize that heresy is a grave offense... But I know...

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: I-I just don't understand why the Order wants us to sing songs written by criminals.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: The more I thought about it, the more out of rhythm I became. In the end, I could barely sing at all.

options.text: But your singing just now was beautiful.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: That song just now? I... I've already forgotten how it's supposed to sound.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Well... what do you think a hymn should sound like?

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: A hymn... as the books state, is a song of praise for the Sentinel who leads the lost to the abundant lands...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: No, not that...  I mean, how does it make you feel? Try to remember what you were feeling when you were singing alone in the corner just now.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: Oh... um... I didn't want to hold everyone back, and I really wanted to help everyone have a great performance at the ceremony.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Then hold onto that feeling. Let's rehearse again! You don't have anyone sitting in on your rehearsals, do you?

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: Huh? No...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Well then, the Laureate and I will be the first to hear you perform!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: As the forgotten songs rise once more, a tale of the past I shall tell...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: This is the story of those who were accused of blasphemy.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: They were once poets, singing songs for the Divinity and the Order. This is how their music became known to all.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Until one day, they stumbled upon a "truth" that could not be proven.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The Sentinel, whom we've always revered, is not what They seem.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The true Divinity granting us blessings is a majestic Celestial Steed that gallops across the high seas and soars through the skies.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: And the teachings in our scriptures? Lies. Lies told to us by the Order.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Ask yourselves this. Why would Imperator, who once sent Rinascita voices from the heavens, allow Their people to suffer aboard those Pilgrim's Sails?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: How could poets who once sang the praises of the Sentinel come to curse and resent Rinascita, a land they loved so much?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: People of Rinascita!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Have you ever wondered why the Order forbids us from seeing their earliest scriptures?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Why did none of the Fools that boarded the Pilgrim's Sail ever thank the Sentinel?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Imperator and Ragunna have always stood united, yet lately... they seem to have vanished from our dreams. Why is that?

items.speaker: Acolyte
items.text: Ciaccona Toccata, in the name of Imperator and Primus Fenrico, you are hereby under arrest. You will face the full weight of the law at the Order Penitentiary.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Is that so? And what are the charges this time, if I may ask?

items.speaker: Acolyte
items.text: Blasphemy!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Oh? The same old tricks again. Is this how the Order intends to keep the truth hidden?

items.speaker: Acolyte
items.text: Hmph. Save your empty words for your confession to the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Acolyte
items.text: Damnation! She's not working alone!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Farewell, Sir Acolyte. Oh, and just a little tip for you. Keep your eyes on the audience below.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Let's see how much of the Order's hypocrisy these "empty words" can unveil...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I have faith the music of the Golden Anthem will one day echo across Ragunna's skies again!

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: "Like the fire that dragged them to hell, these Daemons will spread throughout the land and burn all of Ragunna."

options.text: Is that really going to happen?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Unverifiable, yes, but not entirely dismissible. That said, a peculiar entity did indeed manifest, and it attacked you.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: So, let's start at the very beginning. I have a friend who's an expert in deciphering music. I believe you've met her before...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: She who resembles Topaz should still be near Whisperwind Haven at this hour.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: So... what did you see in Raisa's melody?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The Poets Society was criticized for glorifying the knights over the common folk during one of their hymn performances.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Their assumptions were challenged, so they traveled to the knight's encampment at Whisperwind Haven to verify the truth for themselves.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: This incident may have been the turning point for the Poets Society. Did you find any other clues?

options.text: I strayed into a different melody again.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Next time we venture into the Realm of Melodies, perhaps we should tie our hands together to stop you from getting lost.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: If the Order's choir is pointing us to Whisperwind Haven, then that's where we'll find more clues. Let's go.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: {PlayerName}, {PlayerName}! Are you alright?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Finally, I got you out of there! You must've been drawn into a different melody on our way out.

options.text: (Recount what you saw in the study)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I just walked into a study and ran into a strange little girl. She seemed quite taken with the Poets Society's work.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: She mentioned a name—Ciaccona Toccata

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I see...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I don't know who you met, but Toccata is my family name.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: As I said before... for family matters, let the past stay where it belongs.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Just call me Ciaccona! We don't need to overcomplicate things.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Really, I ended up nowhere in particular.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Hm. Well, we have made some progress. The melody we were in gave me a hint on what to do next.

options.text: Next stop is the Order, right?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Yes. Seeing as they banished the Golden Anthem, we might find more clues in the Cathedral of Mercury.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Music and poetry are born from kindness and hope... yet now they've been twisted to create these monstrosities...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Whoever is behind this won't just sit back and watch.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: ...The thought of confronting them is already inspiring me to write a ballad! What should I call it? Which instruments would fit...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Our enemy hides in the dark, while we remain in the light. If that's the case...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: We need to split up. With your tenacity and Ciaccona's expertise, we will uncover the truth.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Someone has pointed a gun at Cat's Eye. Someone ought to pull back the curtain and expose them.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The family has its ways. Tracing the article's source and finding this mastermind won't be a problem.

options.text: Where should we begin our investigation?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: If we find the <te href=800012>Melody</te> connected to the Poets Society, I can bring the past into clearer focus...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I've got it, {PlayerName}. I know a place where they've definitely left their mark.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Let's go! Onward, to new melodies!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Oh, what splendid singing. Even the Laureate was enraptured!

items.speaker: Choir: Shia
items.text: Raisa did wonderfully!

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: Thanks to you, I've figured it out.

items.speaker: Choir: Raisa
items.text: Sincerity is what truly matters when we pray and sing praises to the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Yes, a hymn isn't just a few lines of history. It is shaped by your feelings and experiences.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: A hymn may differ in each person's eyes, but its resonance and meaning are universal.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I hope you'll give the audience a spectacular performance on the day of the ceremony.
Chorister Raisa leaves to rehearse with the other children. After she leaves, Ciaccona starts asking you questions about what you saw in the melody.

items.speaker: Onlookers
items.text: Laureate, is this all real? What do you mean by the truth? Did you discover something?

items.speaker: Onlookers
items.text: The poets of the Golden Anthem, they...

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: A fascinating story indeed! You've certainly piqued everyone's curiosity.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: This speech you made just now... You're implying the poets of the Golden Anthem were framed and their works deliberately misinterpreted?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Exactly. Someone used the story of the Golden Anthem to stir the public's curiosity. This is what created the Daemons.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: If memory serves, official records of the Order of the Deep state the poets were charged with blasphemy against the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Surely you're not suggesting this was all the Order's doing? I dare say... such remarks would be considered a grave crime punishable by banishment.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Are you from a noble family? The city's elite have a habit of siding with the Order.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Haha, I'm afraid you misunderstand... I admit I do get that question quite often. I am but a traveling playwright.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Your presentation brings me back to a play I wrote in my youth.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: A group of birds locked inside a cage. Convinced they will never fly, they do nothing but stand on a perch.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Then one day, the cage door opened. The young birds flapped their fragile wings attempting to fly, but plummeted to the ground one by one.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Many consider their fate a tragic one. What say you?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: ...I see it differently.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Even if they met their end falling into the abyss, they still had a chance to glimpse the sky and spread their wings.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: And what of those who chose to remain in the cage, having witnessed their predecessors fall to their demise?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Will they still dare to reach for the skies after realizing the cost of choosing to fly? Does the "truth" exist to protect, or to harm? That is the question.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Miss Poet... I look forward to tomorrow's arrival.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I really admire the wind. It's free to go anywhere without restraint. If only it could carry my poems into the hands of everyone across the world.
